data.description:
## Why
A batch failure must NEVER break a stack's internal integrity or partial-land. The culprit stack goes back to its agent; the innocent (independent) stacks proceed.
## What
On combined-batch CI failure, attribute the culprit at STACK granularity, hand that stack back to its owning agent (same contract as the serial failure path), and re-batch the innocent stacks.
## Scope
Phase 2 failure handling. Depends on the batching-assembly + Verified-casting stories.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On batch failure, attribute the culprit at STACK granularity (never evict a change from within a stack); re-run CI on the isolated culprit stack to produce a direct `Verified -1`.
- [ ] Hand the culprit stack back to its owning agent (reuse the serial `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` outcome contract); re-batch the innocent stacks into a fresh combined run.
- [ ] Never partial-land; never break a stack's internal integrity; bounded retries prevent thrash under churn.
data.title: S9 Batch failure: stack-granular attribution + re-batch innocents (Phase 2)
